titleStmt.title: Letter from the Area Administrative Officer to Malachy J. Kelly, 14 March 1916
Letters 1916-1923
titleStmt.author: Area Administrative Officer

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

note.p: This is a letter from the Administrative Area Officer to Malachy Kelly (1850-1916), the Chief Crown Solicitor for Ireland. The letter concerns the injury of Patrick Sheehan (1879-?) a labourer from cork who dislocated his ankle while carrying sacks. The letter inquires as to what procedure should be taken for the situation, it also refers to an enclosed letter from the Federation of Trade Unions Cork and the District Health Insurance Company.

msIdentifier.collection: Chief Secretary's Office Registered Papers, CSO RP/1916/3785

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
Ref No. 92
Subject Injury to Patrick Sheehan. A.A.O., Ireland,
Royal Hos;ital
Dublin
14th March 1916
The Chief Crown Solicitor
Dublin Castle Sir, In reply to your minute of 12/3/16 with
reference to Patrick Sheehan's claim for compensation,
will you please to instruct me as to what procedure I
am now to take in this matter. For your information, I attach copy of letter
received this morning from the Federation of Trade Unions
of Cork and District Health Insurance Society. Yours faithfully,
G. M. Colonel,
Administrative Area Officer
M.R.
